name: Baton Rouge Telco
description_short: Baton Rouge Telco Federal Credit Union is a Louisiana-based credit union established in 1936, serving over 33,900 members with comprehensive banking and lending services.
description_long:
Baton Rouge Telco Federal Credit Union was established in 1936 and is headquartered in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. As the 11th largest credit union in the state, it has grown to serve over 33,900 loyal members with a dedicated staff of 84 employees and manages approximately $449 million in assets. The credit union operates from a single main branch location at 13404 Airline Highway with four ATMs throughout the area.

The institution offers a comprehensive suite of financial products and services including checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), credit cards, auto loans, personal loans, mortgages, and IRAs/KEOGH accounts. Members have access to modern digital banking features such as online banking, mobile app functionality for both iOS and Android, bill pay, remote deposit capture, e-statements, and mobile payments. The credit union also provides wire transfers, financial calculators, and online loan payments, ensuring members can manage their finances conveniently.

Baton Rouge Telco distinguishes itself through its long-standing community presence and commitment to member education. The institution offers financial counseling, financial education programs, and student scholarships to members. The credit union provides a drive-thru teller service and electronic signature authentication for added convenience. As a member-owned financial cooperative, it maintains a personal banking approach focused on serving its specific geographic community and enhancing local financial accessibility.

The credit union has received mixed member reviews, with an average rating of 3.0 out of 5 stars based on available feedback. While some members report positive experiences, noting fair and good service, others have expressed concerns about their experience. Current service status indicates that both the lobby and drive-thru are currently closed, which may impact member access to in-person services. Prospective members should verify current hours and service availability before opening an account.
services: Bill pay and online loan payments
pros: Established institution with 88+ years of history (founded 1936) and significant asset base of $449 million
11th largest credit union in Louisiana, serving 33,900+ members with 84 dedicated employees
Comprehensive product suite including mortgages, auto loans, personal loans, credit cards, and investment accounts
Modern digital banking: mobile apps (iOS/Android), online banking, remote deposit capture, and mobile payments
Member benefits including financial counseling, financial education programs, and student scholarships
Convenient services: 4 ATMs, drive-thru teller access, and electronic signature authentication
Member-owned cooperative structure aligned with local community interests
cons: Limited physical infrastructure with only 1 branch and 4 ATMs, restricting in-person access for members outside immediate area
Both lobby and drive-thru currently closed, creating significant member accessibility challenges
Mixed member satisfaction with average rating of 3.0/5 stars and some negative reviews citing poor experiences
Specific membership eligibility requirements tied to geographic community may restrict who can join
Limited service status transparency and reporting mechanisms for technical issues
best_for: Local Baton Rouge residents seeking a community-focused credit union with long-standing roots and comprehensive banking services
Members who prioritize member-owned institutions offering financial counseling and educational resources
Customers comfortable with primarily digital banking (online/mobile) due to limited physical branch availability
Individuals eligible for membership within Baton Rouge Telco's defined community and geographic service area
diagnosis: Baton Rouge Telco Federal Credit Union is best for established local residents of Baton Rouge seeking comprehensive banking services from a member-owned institution with strong community ties and educational resources. The primary caveat is severely limited physical branch accessibility (1 branch, 4 ATMs, currently closed hours), making it less suitable for members requiring frequent in-person banking, combined with mixed member satisfaction ratings suggesting service quality concerns warrant verification before joining.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
